keyboardChecks.note: TEST RULE ISSUE: The accessibility test rule uses ArrowRight/ArrowLeft for keyboard checks, but the implementation correctly uses ArrowDown/ArrowUp because aria-orientation='vertical'. Per WAI-ARIA Tabs spec, vertical tablists MUST use ArrowDown/ArrowUp, not ArrowRight/ArrowLeft. The implementation is CORRECT.
details: FAIL: TABS-KB-001 - Tests ArrowRight (should test ArrowDown for vertical)
FAIL: TABS-KB-002 - Tests ArrowLeft (should test ArrowUp for vertical)
FAIL: TABS-KB-003 - Tests ArrowLeft wrap (should test ArrowUp for vertical)
FAIL: TABS-KB-004 - Tests ArrowRight wrap (should test ArrowDown for vertical)
implementationVerified: ArrowDown moves to next tab with wrapping (line 120-123)
ArrowUp moves to previous tab with wrapping (line 124-127)
Home key jumps to first tab (line 128-131)
End key jumps to last tab (line 132-135)

issues.description: Accessibility test rule uses incorrect keyboard checks for vertical tabs
issues.details: The accessibility test rule checks for ArrowRight/ArrowLeft navigation (TABS-KB-001 through TABS-KB-004), but the Tabs block correctly implements ArrowDown/ArrowUp because the tablist has aria-orientation='vertical'. Per WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices, vertical tablists MUST use ArrowDown/ArrowUp, not ArrowRight/ArrowLeft. The block implementation is correct; the test rule needs updating.
issues.fix: Update .taktician/instructions/accessibility-rules/tabs.json keyboard_checks to test ArrowDown/ArrowUp instead of ArrowRight/ArrowLeft for vertical tabs. Add orientation detection to keyboard checks or create separate rule variants for horizontal vs vertical tabs.
issues.impact: Test reports false failures for correct implementation. Blocks that implement vertical tabs correctly will show keyboard check failures even though they pass WAI-ARIA requirements.
issues.note: This does not affect the Tabs block's production readiness. The implementation is correct per WAI-ARIA spec.

issues.description: React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies in Tabs.tsx
issues.details: React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'attributes.tabsColor', 'attributes.tabsDescription', 'attributes.tabsHeading', 'innerBlocksLength', and 'setAttributes'. This is a common pattern in the theme when syncing attributes from child blocks.
issues.fix: Add missing dependencies to the useEffect dependency array, or add eslint-disable comment if intentional.
issues.impact: Potential stale closure issues if dependencies change without re-running effect. However, this pattern is used consistently across other blocks in the theme.
